# PUBLICATION_POSITION_ANALYSIS

**Document ID:** A66-PUBLICATION-POSITION

**Title:** Publication Position Analysis

**Document Class:** Investigation Artifact

**Status:** Stable

**Author:** SvitloSk Project — Dual Kernel Investigation

---

# Purpose

This document determines whether Publication belongs to Domain Kernel, Architecture Kernel, Both, or Neither.

---

# Publication Position Investigation

## Evidence from GLOSSARY.md

**Definition:** "A public information message generated by the SvitloSk system for distribution through one or more supported communication channels"

**Analysis:**

- "public information message" — DOMAIN concept (information for public)
- "generated by the SvitloSk system" — ARCHITECTURE concept (system-generated)
- "for distribution" — DOMAIN concept (distribution purpose)
- "through supported communication channels" — ARCHITECTURE concept (channels)

**Classification:** MIXED — Publication contains both Domain and Architecture elements

---

## Evidence from DATA_MODEL.md

**Definition:** "Logical representation of interpreted information prepared for public distribution"

**Analysis:**

- "Logical representation" — ARCHITECTURE concept (data model)
- "interpreted information" — DOMAIN concept (interpreted data)
- "prepared for public distribution" — DOMAIN concept (distribution purpose)

**Classification:** MIXED — Publication contains both Domain and Architecture elements

---

## Evidence from TJS-000 §4

**Definition:** "Publication represents one independent publication belonging to an Issue"

**Analysis:**

- "independent publication" — DOMAIN concept (publication unit)
- "belonging to an Issue" — DOMAIN concept (editorial relationship)

**Classification:** DOMAIN — pure domain concept

---

## Evidence from TJS-021 §4

**Definition:** "Every Publication SHALL pass through one or more of the following states: Generated, Published, Updated, Archived, Removed"

**Analysis:**

- "pass through states" — ARCHITECTURE concept (lifecycle)
- "Generated, Published, Updated, Archived, Removed" — ARCHITECTURE concept (states)

**Classification:** ARCHITECTURE — lifecycle concept

---

## Evidence from TJS-010 §3.3

**Data Flow:** "External Data Sources → Parser → Normalized Dataset → Publication Engine → Publication Package → Telegram Publisher → Telegram Channel → Subscribers"

**Analysis:**

- Publication appears in data flow as output of Publication Engine
- Publication is consumed by Telegram Publisher
- This is ARCHITECTURE context

**Classification:** ARCHITECTURE — data flow concept

---

## Evidence from CASE-001F

**Finding:** "Publication is a FUNDAMENTAL domain concept that CANNOT be removed"

**Analysis:**

- Publication has real-world meaning (public information message)
- Publication exists independently of implementation
- Publication is the semantic center of the ontology

**Classification:** DOMAIN — fundamental domain concept

---

# Position Summary

| Evidence Source | Classification | Reason |
|-----------------|----------------|--------|
| GLOSSARY.md definition | MIXED | Contains both Domain and Architecture elements |
| DATA_MODEL.md definition | MIXED | Contains both Domain and Architecture elements |
| TJS-000 §4 | DOMAIN | Pure domain concept |
| TJS-021 §4 | ARCHITECTURE | Lifecycle concept |
| TJS-010 §3.3 | ARCHITECTURE | Data flow concept |
| CASE-001F | DOMAIN | Fundamental domain concept |

---

# Publication Classification

## Is Publication Domain?

**Evidence FOR:**

- Publication has real-world meaning (public information message)
- Publication exists independently of implementation
- Publication is the semantic center of the ontology
- Publication cannot be removed (CASE-001F)
- TJS-000 §4 defines Publication as domain concept

**Evidence AGAINST:**

- Publication is "generated by the SvitloSk system" (architecture)
- Publication has lifecycle states (architecture)
- Publication appears in data flow (architecture)

---

## Is Publication Architecture?

**Evidence FOR:**

- Publication is "generated by the SvitloSk system" (architecture)
- Publication has lifecycle states (architecture)
- Publication appears in data flow (architecture)
- Publication is produced by Publication Engine (architecture)

**Evidence AGAINST:**

- Publication has real-world meaning (domain)
- Publication exists independently of implementation (domain)
- Publication is the semantic center (domain)
- Publication cannot be removed (domain)

---

# Final Classification

**Publication exists in BOTH kernels.**

**Publication is a BRIDGE concept that spans Domain and Architecture.**

**Publication is the point where Domain and Architecture meet.**

---

# Implications

## For Domain Kernel

Publication provides the domain meaning: "public information message for residents"

## For Architecture Kernel

Publication provides the architectural output: "deterministic output from Publication Engine"

## For Dual Kernel Model

Publication is the CONCEPTUAL BRIDGE between Domain and Architecture.

---

# Other Concepts Position

| Concept | Domain? | Architecture? | Position |
|---------|---------|---------------|----------|
| Issue | PARTIALLY | NO | Domain (editorial concept) |
| Journal | YES | NO | Domain (continuous publication) |
| Publication Package | NO | YES | Architecture (collection) |
| Schedule | YES | NO | Domain (outage information) |
| Telegram Message | NO | YES | Architecture (platform artifact) |
| Address | YES | NO | Domain (location) |
| Territory | YES | NO | Domain (geographic) |
| Community | YES | NO | Domain (geographic scope) |
| Parser | NO | YES | Architecture (component) |
| Publication Engine | NO | YES | Architecture (component) |

---

# Key Insight

**Publication is the ONLY concept that exists in BOTH kernels.**

**Publication is the bridge between Domain and Architecture.**

**This explains why Publication is so central to the ontology.**

---

# End of Publication Position Analysis
